compiled_state.title: [build-review-agents.sh]: Classifier JSON output empty -> json.load fails with JSONDecodeError when parsing classifier output
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Expected Behavior
The review classifier agent returns valid JSON that build-review-agents.sh can parse to select the review tier.

## Actual Behavior
The classifier fails to return parseable JSON, causing the inline python3 parser to fail with:

REVIEW_TIER=standard REVIEW_AGENT=dso:code-reviewer-standard
CLASSIFIER_OUTPUT:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
    import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(json.dumps({k: d[k] for k in ["selected_tier","size_action","diff_size_lines"] if k in d}, indent=2))
  File "json/__init__.py", line 298, in load
    return loads(fp.read(), ...)
json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)

The script exits 1 with an empty CLASSIFIER_OUTPUT, suggesting the classifier agent returned no JSON output or was interrupted before producing valid output.

## Investigation Notes
- REVIEW_TIER defaults to standard when classifier fails, so the review still runs
- The error occurs when json.load(sys.stdin) receives empty stdin from the classifier pipeline
- Root cause: classifier agent returns empty or non-JSON response

## Fix Directions
1. Add a guard in build-review-agents.sh to handle empty/invalid classifier output gracefully (fail-open to standard tier with a warning)
2. Investigate why the classifier agent returns empty output (LLM behavioral or tool call failure)
